rootCauseClusters.name: Unguarded Concurrent Execution in PollingTimer
rootCauseClusters.description: PollingTimer has no mutex or isTickInFlight flag. forceRefresh() bypasses isRunning guard. Concurrent tick() calls leak duplicate timer chains and corrupt shared state.

rootCauseClusters.name: EMA Burn Rate Drift from Duplicate Calculations
rootCauseClusters.description: refreshStatusBar() unconditionally recalculates burn rate EMA on every call (API poll, API error, cache watcher). Without new JSONL data, repeated EMA steps decay burn rate toward zero.

rootCauseClusters.name: Incomplete Signature Hash Blocks Valid Updates
rootCauseClusters.description: Status bar signature hash includes only rate limit percentages and cooldowns. Cost, token count, burn rate, and file count changes are silently blocked from rendering.

findings.description: forceRefresh() calls tick() without checking isRunning or whether a tick is already in-flight. Two concurrent tick() calls produce duplicate timer chains (leaked setTimeout references) and doubled API calls.
findings.suggestion: Add an isTickInFlight boolean flag. Set true before fetchFn(), set false after. forceRefresh() should check isRunning and wait for or skip in-flight ticks.

findings.description: If forceRefresh() is called after stop() (e.g. during deactivation race), tick() runs, onData fires, and usageCache!.writeCache() dereferences null usageCache (set to null in deactivate()). The non-null assertion throws TypeError.
findings.suggestion: forceRefresh() should check isRunning before calling tick(). Additionally, the onData callback should guard usageCache with optional chaining.

findings.description: refreshStatusBar() calls calculateBurnRateEMA() and replaces burnRateTracker on every invocation, even without new JSONL data. Each API poll/error/cache event applies an extra EMA smoothing step, decaying burn rate toward zero during normal operation.
findings.suggestion: Only recalculate burn rate EMA in the SessionWatcher onUpdate callback (when new JSONL data arrives). In refreshStatusBar(), reuse the last computed burn rate instead of recalculating.

findings.description: Signature hash excludes todayCost, monthCost, burnRate, totalInputTokens, totalOutputTokens. When JSONL data changes these values without crossing a percentage rounding boundary, the update() method returns early. Tooltip shows stale cost/token data.
findings.suggestion: Include todayCost, monthCost, and burnRate in the signature. Or compute signature from all displayed fields.

findings.description: readCache() does new Date(parsed.apiUsage.fetchedAt) without validating the field exists. If undefined, produces Invalid Date. getStaleness() handles this (returns 'critical') but statusBar.ts tooltip computes NaN age, displaying 'API data is NaNm old'.
findings.suggestion: Validate fetchedAt exists and is a valid date string before the Date constructor. If invalid, set fetchedAt to null or current date.

findings.description: isFresh() method is defined but never called anywhere in the codebase.
findings.suggestion: Remove it. If needed later, re-add with actual callers.

findings.description: pollingTimer.start() fires before performInitialParse() completes. First API result (5s) may arrive when lastKnownBuckets is still null. refreshStatusBar() silently returns, losing the display opportunity. Next API data arrives at 120s.
findings.suggestion: Start pollingTimer after performInitialParse completes, or set lastKnownBuckets from cached store data before starting the timer.

findings.description: vscode.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher with RelativePattern on non-workspace path (~/.claude/cache/) has inconsistent behavior across platforms. May not fire on some VS Code versions or with remote file systems.
findings.suggestion: Consider a fallback: if no file change event arrives within 2x the expected polling interval, do a manual readCache() check.

planAttacks.attack: The architecture introduces 9 module-level mutable variables with no synchronization, creating a large surface for race conditions between SessionWatcher, PollingTimer, and cache watcher callbacks.
planAttacks.defense: JavaScript is single-threaded; all callbacks run on the event loop. True data races are impossible. The only issue is interleaved async operations, which the isRunning guard in PollingTimer handles for the deactivation case.

planAttacks.attack: Burn rate EMA is recalculated on every refreshStatusBar() call, not just on new data arrival. This silently corrupts a key metric during normal operation.
planAttacks.defense: The EMA converges to the same value when called with unchanged data. But the time-dependent denominator (timeSpanMinutes) changes between calls, causing the rate to drift.

planAttacks.attack: The signature hash optimization blocks legitimate updates to cost and token data in the tooltip.
planAttacks.defense: The status bar text only shows percentages and cooldowns, which are in the signature. The tooltip is only built on hover... but actually, the MarkdownString is pre-computed in update() and cached.

planAttacks.attack: Shared cache via filesystem is fragile - file watchers may not fire, atomic writes can fail, and format versioning is missing.
planAttacks.defense: Filesystem IPC is the only reliable cross-window communication in VS Code extensions. Atomic write + EPERM retry handles Windows edge cases. The feature degrades gracefully (each window works independently).

planAttacks.attack: The staleness system may show perpetual 'critical' dimming if the first API fetch fails and never recovers, making the status bar permanently gray.
planAttacks.defense: Exponential backoff caps at 300s, so recovery attempts continue indefinitely. The critical color is intentionally attention-grabbing to signal the API is unreachable.
